text: Dogs are domesticated mammals belonging to the family Canidae.
The domestic dog was one of the first animals to be domesticated by humans.
Dogs have an exceptional sense of smell, with approximately 300 million olfactory receptors.
A dog's hearing range extends to frequencies as high as 65,000 Hz.
The average lifespan of a dog is between 10 and 13 years.
Golden retrievers are known for their friendly and tolerant attitudes.
German shepherds are commonly used as police and military working dogs.
Labrador retrievers have been the most popular dog breed in America for decades.
Dogs are omnivores and can digest a variety of foods including meat and vegetables.
A dog's nose print is unique, similar to a human fingerprint.
Puppies are born blind and deaf, with their senses developing over the first few weeks.
Dogs can be trained to detect diseases such as cancer and diabetes.
The basenji is known as the barkless dog because it produces an unusual yodel-like sound.
Greyhounds are the fastest dog breed and can reach speeds of 45 miles per hour.
Dogs have three eyelids: an upper lid, a lower lid, and a nictitating membrane.
The Chihuahua is the smallest recognized dog breed in the world.
Great Danes are among the tallest dog breeds, often exceeding 30 inches at the shoulder.
Dogs sweat primarily through their paw pads rather than through their skin.
Border collies are often considered the most intelligent dog breed.
Dogs have approximately 1,700 taste buds compared to humans who have about 9,000.
The Akita is a large and powerful dog breed originating from Japan.
Dogs can understand up to 250 words and gestures with proper training.
Dalmatians are born completely white and develop their spots as they mature.
Bloodhounds have the most powerful sense of smell of any dog breed.
Dogs have dichromatic vision and see primarily in shades of blue and yellow.
The Siberian husky was bred to pull sleds in cold climates.
Dogs have 42 teeth as adults, compared to 28 teeth in puppies.
The poodle originated in Germany as a water retriever despite its French association.
Dogs sleep an average of 12 to 14 hours per day.
The Australian shepherd was actually developed in the United States, not Australia.
Dogs can rotate their ears independently to better locate sounds.
The Newfoundland dog has webbed feet and is an excellent swimmer.
A dog's normal body temperature ranges from 101 to 102.5 degrees Fahrenheit.
Bulldogs were originally bred for bull-baiting in medieval England.
Dogs have a specialized reflective layer behind their retinas called the tapetum lucidum.
The Shiba Inu is one of the oldest and smallest native Japanese dog breeds.
Dogs mark their territory using scent glands located in their paws.
The Irish wolfhound is one of the tallest dog breeds in existence.
Dogs can detect seizures in humans before they occur through scent changes.
The Bernese mountain dog originated in the Swiss Alps as a farm dog.
Dogs have a vomeronasal organ that detects pheromones.
Beagles have approximately 220 million scent receptors in their noses.
The dachshund was bred to hunt badgers in their underground burrows.
Dogs dream during REM sleep, similar to humans.
The Rhodesian ridgeback was bred to hunt lions in Africa.
Dogs have a wet nose to help absorb scent chemicals from the air.
The Cavalier King Charles spaniel is named after King Charles II of England.
Dogs can hear sounds four times farther away than humans can.
The Portuguese water dog was bred to assist fishermen along the coast.
Dogs have 18 muscles controlling each ear.
The Weimaraner is known for its distinctive silver-gray coat.
Dogs communicate through body language, vocalizations, and scent marking.
The Saint Bernard was bred for rescue work in the Swiss Alps.
Dogs have a higher metabolism than humans relative to their body size.
The Rottweiler descended from Roman drover dogs used to herd cattle.
Dogs can smell fear and anxiety in humans through chemical changes.
The Cocker Spaniel was originally bred as a hunting dog for woodcock.
Dogs experience rapid eye movement sleep and may twitch during dreams.
The Doberman pinscher was developed by a German tax collector for protection.
Dogs have approximately 319 bones in their bodies.
The Boxer was named for its tendency to stand on hind legs and paw.
Dogs can be left or right pawed, similar to human handedness.
The Maltese is one of the oldest toy breeds, dating back over 2000 years.
Dogs have a special membrane that protects their eyes while swimming.
The Yorkshire terrier was originally bred to catch rats in textile mills.
Dogs can sense changes in barometric pressure before storms arrive.
The Alaskan malamute is one of the oldest Arctic sled dog breeds.
Dogs have scent glands between their toes that leave chemical markers.
The French bulldog originated as a miniaturized English bulldog.
Dogs can remember events and commands for up to five minutes without reinforcement.
The Jack Russell terrier was developed for fox hunting in England.
Dogs have a remarkable ability to read human facial expressions.
The Pomeranian descended from large sled dogs of the Arctic regions.
Dogs pant to regulate their body temperature through evaporative cooling.
The Boston terrier is known as the American Gentleman due to its tuxedo markings.
Dogs have been selectively bred for over 15,000 years by humans.
The Samoyed has a distinctive smile that prevents drooling and icicle formation.
Dogs can learn to respond to over 1,000 different words.
The miniature schnauzer was bred as a ratting dog on German farms.
Dogs have a sense of time and can anticipate regular events like feeding.
The English springer spaniel is named for its hunting technique of springing game.
Dogs can detect human emotions through changes in body odor.
The Vizsla is a Hungarian hunting dog known for its golden rust coat.
Dogs have a third eyelid that helps keep the eye moist and protected.
The Bichon Frise was popular among European royalty in the 16th century.
Dogs can track scents that are weeks old under the right conditions.
The Brittany spaniel is an energetic hunting dog from northwestern France.
Dogs yawn as a calming signal and to relieve stress.
The Chinese crested dog can be either hairless or have a full coat.
Dogs have a jacobson organ that processes pheromone signals.
The Papillon is named for its butterfly-shaped ears.
Dogs can be trained to detect bed bugs with over 95% accuracy.
The Airedale terrier is the largest of the terrier breeds.
Dogs evolved from wolves approximately 20,000 to 40,000 years ago.
The Shetland sheepdog was bred to herd sheep in the Shetland Islands.
Dogs have four times as many neurons in their cerebral cortex as cats.
